page: Amazon Web Services m8i.8xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by a large 480 MB L3 cache. Memory configuration consists of 128.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 7 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

The hardware architecture includes advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512, AVX_VNNI), which accelerate machine learning and cryptographic workloads. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in text processing and small-model LLM text generation. Strong performance is also observed in memory bandwidth for large blocks, single- and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Redis SET operations. Standard database operations and software compilation benchmarks fall within the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8i.8xlarge offers a balanced resource density suitable for enterprise workloads that require dedicated compute power without the premium of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The inclusion of DDR5 memory and advanced CPU instructions allows it to handle vector and matrix operations efficiently on standard compute resources.

This server is well-suited for workloads such as web serving, Redis caching, text processing, and medium-scale language model inference. It also fits general-purpose enterprise applications, database operations, and moderate computational tasks that benefit from dedicated Intel Xeon cores.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 128.0 GB of DDR5 RAM at 7 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of memory per core. The CPU features a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it provides a 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks indicate top-tier performance in text processing and small-model LLM text generation, alongside strong results in Redis operations and large-block memory bandwidth. This configuration is highly efficient for general-purpose enterprise applications, web serving, Redis caching, and moderate machine learning inference workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 15 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.8xlarge features 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 15 Gbps network bandwidth for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, DDR5 memory, and advanced matrix acceleration for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor at 3.9 GHz.
128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 RAM providing 4.0 GB per core.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix and vector math.
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration.
Top-tier benchmark performance in text processing and small-model LLM inference.
Optimized for web serving, Redis caching, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
